name: forgejo-keycloak-rust-mcp
description: A clean-room Rust MCP gateway for Forgejo with Keycloak identity, explicit operation authorization, approval gates, bounded responses, and Forgejo ACL enforcement.
description_good_justification: The public README describes the gateway, its trust model, current scope, and intentionally disabled operations: https://codeberg.org/GetOpir/forgejo-keycloak-rust-mcp/src/branch/main/README.md
interact_justification: The public repository provides installation, issue reporting, security reporting, and contribution paths: https://codeberg.org/GetOpir/forgejo-keycloak-rust-mcp
contribution_justification: The contribution process, tests, licensing, and AI-assisted contribution policy are documented: https://codeberg.org/GetOpir/forgejo-keycloak-rust-mcp/src/branch/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
contribution_requirements_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md defines focused changes, required tests, documentation updates, security rules, and license terms: https://codeberg.org/GetOpir/forgejo-keycloak-rust-mcp/src/branch/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
floss_license_justification: The project is released under AGPL-3.0-or-later: https://codeberg.org/GetOpir/forgejo-keycloak-rust-mcp/src/branch/main/LICENSE
license_location_justification: The root LICENSE, LICENSES directory, REUSE.toml, and per-file SPDX metadata provide license information: https://codeberg.org/GetOpir/forgejo-keycloak-rust-mcp/src/branch/main/REUSE.toml
documentation_basics_justification: README.md and docs/install.md document prerequisites, installation, configuration, and operation.
documentation_interface_justification: The MCP operations and request fields are documented in docs/mcp-functions.md: https://codeberg.org/GetOpir/forgejo-keycloak-rust-mcp/src/branch/main/docs/mcp-functions.md
sites_https_justification: The public repository, releases, issues, and wiki are hosted over HTTPS at Codeberg.
repo_public_justification: The complete source and history are publicly readable: https://codeberg.org/GetOpir/forgejo-keycloak-rust-mcp
repo_track_justification: Git records changes, authorship, history, tags, and branches in the public repository.
repo_interim_justification: The public main branch contains changes between tagged releases.
repo_distributed_justification: The project uses Git and is clonable from its public Codeberg repository.
version_unique_justification: Cargo package versions and annotated release tags uniquely identify releases.
version_semver_justification: Published crates and release tags use semantic versioning.
version_tags_justification: Public releases have corresponding annotated Git tags: https://codeberg.org/GetOpir/forgejo-keycloak-rust-mcp/tags
release_notes_justification: Hosted releases and docs/release-notes summarize release changes: https://codeberg.org/GetOpir/forgejo-keycloak-rust-mcp/releases
release_notes_vulns_justification: Release notes identify security-related changes; the security policy defines private vulnerability handling.
report_process_justification: Issues can be filed in the public Codeberg issue tracker: https://codeberg.org/GetOpir/forgejo-keycloak-rust-mcp/issues
report_tracker_justification: Codeberg publicly archives and tracks issue status: https://codeberg.org/GetOpir/forgejo-keycloak-rust-mcp/issues
report_archive_justification: Public issues remain archived in the Codeberg tracker.
vulnerability_report_process_justificati: SECURITY.md directs reports to the monitored info@getopir.com role address, prohibits public disclosure, commits to acknowledgement within seven calendar days, and documents restricted handling: https://codeberg.org/GetOpir/forgejo-keycloak-rust-mcp/src/branch/main/SECURITY.md
build_justification: Cargo.lock and the Cargo workspace provide a documented, repeatable build invocation.
build_common_tools_justification: The project builds with the standard Rust Cargo toolchain.
build_floss_tools_justification: The Rust compiler and Cargo build tool are FLOSS.
test_justification: The Cargo workspace includes automated tests for identity, policy, approval, audit, principal mapping, Forgejo response handling, and rate limiting.
test_continuous_integration_justificatio: Forgejo Actions runs format, check, test, secret scanning, dependency audit, dependency policy, and SBOM checks on changes.
test_policy_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md requires tests when behavior changes.
tests_are_added_justification: Behavior changes in the maintained source include focused regression tests.
warnings_justification: CI runs cargo check and strict Clippy across the workspace and all targets.
warnings_fixed_justification: Push and pull-request CI reject every Clippy warning with -D warnings.
warnings_strict_justification: Both internal Forgejo and public Codeberg CI run cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings.
no_leaked_credentials_justification: Checksum-verified Gitleaks scans full Git history in CI, and SECURITY.md forbids committed credentials.
crypto_keylength_justification: OIDC discovery fails startup unless signing keys use an explicit approved asymmetric algorithm, RSA keys are at least 2048 bits, EC algorithms match P-256 or P-384, and EdDSA uses Ed25519.
static_analysis_justification: CI runs strict Clippy source analysis, RustSec cargo-audit, cargo-deny, and Gitleaks.
static_analysis_fixed_justification: The normal change workflow requires the automated security and policy checks to pass.
static_analysis_often_justification: Strict Clippy source analysis runs automatically on every push and pull request.
